layout.spacing: Dense and compact. Tailwind p-4 to p-6 for widgets.

surface_styles.dashboard_cards: Flat background, 1px subtle border (border-border), no shadow, rounded-none or max rounded-md.

interactions.hover_states: Hard background shifts. hover:bg-zinc-100 dark:hover:bg-zinc-800. Text must remain accessible.
interactions.buttons: Square or rounded-sm edges. Sharp hover transitions.
interactions.loading: Terminal-style blinking cursor or high-contrast skeleton loaders.

shadcn_customization.button: rounded-none or rounded-sm, uppercase mono text if technical.
shadcn_customization.input_textarea: Focus state must have ring-2 ring-primary ring-offset-background. Sharp borders.
shadcn_customization.cards: Remove default heavy shadows. Use border-collapse style or sharp 1px border.

d3_graph.nodes: Circles with stroke. Color coded based on entity type or suspicion level.
d3_graph.links: Thin lines with low opacity. Red lines for suspicious relationships.
d3_graph.background: Transparent to blend with the card background.
d3_graph.interactions: Zoom, pan, drag nodes, hover tooltips.

images.description: Typography close up for empty states or branding.

testing.guidelines: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid="analyze-button"`, `data-testid="score-result"`).

instructions_to_main_agent: Implement a responsive Control Room Grid layout. The main area should be split between the Input Form, the Results Panel (Score + Explanation), and the D3.js Graph.
Use IBM Plex Sans for all text, and IBM Plex Mono for data labels, scores, and entity types.
D3 Graph must have a transparent background and color-coded nodes (Red=Fake Source/Suspicious, Blue=Trustworthy, Yellow=Suspicious). Include zoom and pan behaviors.
Support Dark/Light mode natively using Tailwind 'dark:' classes.
Avoid round buttons. Use `rounded-none` or `rounded-sm` for a sharp, precise, 'Swiss Brutalist' data aesthetic.
Add 'data-testid' to all buttons, form inputs, score readouts, and toggle switches.
The UI should feel authoritative and objective. Emphasize contrast and precise borders. Use negative space inside cards instead of drop shadows.
